mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a key psychological and historical level, it signals a restrictive monetary policy regime that increases the cost of capital and slows economic activity, leading to negative equity returns as future earnings are discounted more heavily.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has been in a tightening cycle since mid-2004.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, supporting positive equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth for over two years.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where the 3-month change in PAYEMS is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where housing starts (HOUST) remain strong while retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates indicates an economic imbalance. The capital-intensive housing sector is running ahead of underlying consumer demand, signaling a future contraction and negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market has been exceptionally strong, raising concerns of a bubble.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, a sharp acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals the economy is overheating. This leads to input shortages, rising cost pressures, and prompts preemptive monetary tightening, all of which are negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The economy is widely considered to be in a mid-to-late stage expansion.
failure_condition: The correlation between the signal and 20-day forward returns is positive over the subsequent 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation (CPIAUCSL) while the Fed is actively raising rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a powerful headwind for equities. This combination ensures policy will remain restrictive or become even more so, compressing valuation multiples.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI has been trending up, and the Fed is in a clear hiking cycle.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger is active fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below a level consistent with full employment (approx. 5.0-5.2%), it signals rising wage pressures and prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy to preempt inflation, which is negative for stock market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE is 5.0%, at or near many estimates of the natural rate.
failure_condition: In the 12 months following instances where UNRATE drops below 5.2%, the S&P 500 total return is positive more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, negative month-over-month change in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a high-frequency leading indicator of a slowdown in a key cyclical sector, causing an immediate negative reaction in equity markets due to its broad economic impact.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a major driver of the current economic cycle.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Higher year-over-year growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is a direct measure of a strengthening economy. This translates into higher aggregate corporate earnings, justifying higher equity valuations and driving positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Real GDP has been growing at a solid pace since the 2001 recession.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between GDPC1 YoY growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.10 over any 5-year rolling period.
